messages.content:
Which of the following is characteristic of B- but not T-cells?

Select one:

a.
CD3


b.
Class I MHC


c.
Measles virus receptor


d.
Polyclonal activation by concanavalin A (ConA)


e.
Transmembrane surface immunoglobulin
e. Transmembrane surface immunoglobulin

Which of the following statements is TRUE of CD4?

Select one:

a.
CD4 is highly polymorphic


b.
CD4 is heterodimeric


c.
CD4 binds processed peptide in its outer groove


d.
CD4 is essentially an intracellular glycoprotein


e.
CD4 binds to MHC class II on antigen-presenting cells

e.
CD4 binds to MHC class II on antigen-presenting cells

A hapten is best defined as being:

Select one:

a.
A paratope


b.
An immunogen


c.
An epitope


d.
A small chemical grouping that reacts with preformed antibodies


e.
A carrier

d.
A small chemical grouping that reacts with preformed antibodies

Antibody molecules

Select one:

a.
Are globular proteins found predominantly in the gamma region during electrophoresis


b.
Use the Fc region to bind antigen


c.
Are produced by T cells


d.
Recognise carbohydrates via a lectin head

a.
Are globular proteins found predominantly in the gamma region during electrophoresis

IgA


a.
Neutralizes pathogen and toxins

Neutralizes pathogen and toxins


b.
Serves as a major protective defense of the mucosal surfaces of the body


c.
Activates complement


d.
act as an opsonin


e.
Dimer


f.
Pentamer


g.
act as mediator of antibody- dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity


h.
Exists in 2 isotypes

b. Serves as a major protective defense of the mucosal surfaces of the body

e. Dimer

h. Exists in 2 isotypes

Ig idiotypes are found:

Select one:

a.
In the variable region of both heavy and light chains


b.
In the constant region of the heavy chain


c.
In the hinge region


d.
In the constant region of the light chain


e.
Only in the light chain
a. In the variable region of both heavy and light chains

A fragment antigen binding (Fab) fragment:

Select one:

a.
Lacks light chains


b.
Has no interchain disulfide bonds


c.
Is produced by separation of heavy and light chains


d.
Is produced by pepsin treatment


e.
Binds antigen
e. Binds antigen

IgM


a.
Acts as an opsonin, enhancing phagocytosis


b.
Monomer


c.
Possesses the highest avidity 


d.
Dimer


e.
Pentamer


f.
Activates complement


g.
Transported across the placenta


h.
 Acts as mediator of antibody- dependent cell-mediated cytotoxicity

c. Possesses the highest avidity

e. Pentamer

f. Activates complement

Antibodies recognize antigens

Select one:

a.
Can neutralise pathogens within host cells


b.
Have a similar high binding affinity regardless of their isotype


c.
Via their hypervariable regions


d.
By covalent bonding to specific epitopes
c. Via their hypervariable regions

Which of the following statements does NOT apply to IgG?

Select one:

a.
Appears early in the primary immune response


b.
Neutralizes bacterial toxins


c.
Can fix complement


d.
Crosses the human placenta


e.
Opsonizes bacteria
a. Appears early in the primary immune response

Which of the following statements does NOT apply to IgG?

Select one:

a.
Appears early in the primary immune response


b.
Neutralizes bacterial toxins


c.
Can fix complement


d.
Crosses the human placenta


e.
Opsonizes bacteria
a. Appears early in the primary immune response
